summary: A fortified military structure with a distinctive cross-shaped floor plan featuring two tall corner towers with crenellated roofs, connected by lower defensive walls. The building uses sandstone blocks for walls with brick accents, white concrete or quartz for trim and coping, and includes small window openings. A perimeter fence of white blocks surrounds the entire compound.

shape.overall_form: cross-shaped with two prominent corner towers

rebuild_hints: Build two tall towers first (approximately 12 blocks high) at opposite corners
Connect towers with lower walls forming a cross pattern
Add white quartz/concrete coping along all wall tops
Place brick accent band below the crenellated roof edges
Install small window openings (1-2 blocks) at regular intervals on tower sides
Create perimeter fence using white concrete blocks spaced 1 block apart
Ensure flat roofs on towers with crenellated edges (alternating blocks)
Interior courtyard area should be open brown dirt/coarse dirt
uncertainties: Exact interior room layout not visible from exterior views
Precise block count for tower height (estimated 12 blocks)
Whether windows are glass panes or empty openings
Exact material of white trim blocks (quartz vs concrete vs bone block)
Interior floor materials and room divisions
Presence of stairs or internal structures within towers
